levels.description: The process is not implemented or fails to achieve its purpose. There is little or no evidence of any systematic achievement of the process purpose.
characteristics: Process does not exist or is not implemented
No evidence of consistent process outcomes
Objectives are not being achieved
Process activities may be performed sporadically

levels.description: The implemented process achieves its process purpose. Basic practices are performed and work products are produced, but there may not be rigorous planning and tracking.
characteristics: Process purpose is being achieved
Basic practices are performed
Work products exist but may not be controlled
Performance is dependent on individual knowledge
Informal approaches may be used

levels.description: The process is implemented in a managed fashion (planned, monitored, and adjusted) and its work products are appropriately established, controlled, and maintained.
characteristics: Process is planned and tracked
Work products are identified and controlled
Performance is managed against defined objectives
Responsibilities are assigned and communicated
Adequate resources and information are provided
Stakeholder needs are identified and managed

levels.description: The managed process is implemented using a defined process that is capable of achieving its process outcomes. Standard processes exist and are used as the basis for process implementations.
characteristics: Standard process is defined and documented
Process is deployed based on organizational standards
Process interactions and dependencies are defined
Competency requirements are identified
Infrastructure requirements are identified
Process data is collected for analysis

levels.description: The established process operates within defined limits to achieve its process outcomes. Quantitative management needs are identified, measurement data is collected and analyzed to identify assignable causes of variation.
characteristics: Quantitative process objectives are defined
Measurement data is systematically collected
Statistical techniques are applied to manage performance
Process performance is predictable
Special causes of variation are identified and corrected
Process outcomes meet quantitative quality objectives

levels.description: The predictable process is continually improved to meet relevant current and projected business goals. Improvement objectives are defined, quantified, and implemented.
characteristics: Process improvement is driven by quantitative analysis
Innovation opportunities are systematically identified
Impact of proposed changes is assessed quantitatively
Implementation of improvements is managed
Improvement effectiveness is evaluated
Continuous optimization is embedded in culture
